text:
It was just after dawn. Oliver was weaving through the dew-speckled trees near his ancestral castle, his feet barely touching the soft earth.

"Good morning," he whispered.

A crow answered him.

Something felt off. That wasn’t just a bird. Something grew giant in the tall oak beside it.

Turning sharply, Oliver saw it—it was a colossal owl, its eyes gleaming menacingly.

Oliver leaped back. Maybe it hadn’t wanted him there?

"Not tonight?" he pleaded.

Just as the owl made a great swooping motion—

It flew away.

The trees whispered. That night, Oliver slept peacefully.

A month later.

He wandered back.

Something was different. It was warmer. The woods smelled just right.

Turning a corner, Oliver stumbled over a tiny owl owl.

Its feathers were ruffled.

"Oh."

It flew away.

That night—

Something glittered—

He blinked it away. Just an ancient crystal.

Oliver smiled. "That’s better."

That
